channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Multiscale-structured miniaturized 3D force sensors
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 18 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02508-7

Tactile sensing to differentiate normal and shear forces remains a challenge. A force microsensor array based on graphene and a liquid-metal composites decouples normal–shear force sensing, achieving a 200 μm scale and a 0.9 μN force detection limit.
item.dc:title: Multiscale-structured miniaturized 3D force sensors
item.dc:creator: Guolin Yun
Zesheng Chen
Zhuo Chen
Jinrui Chen
Binghan Zhou
Mingfei Xiao
Michael Stevens
Manish Chhowalla
Tawfique Hasan

item.title: Reconstructing into a denser phase
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 13 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02502-z

Tellurium vacancies can drive atomic reconstruction in PdTe2 thin films, converting them into non-layered PdTe. Such a non-stoichiometric phase transition yields large-scale thin-film superconductors and can also create an intermediate heterostructure that enables helicity-sensitive terahertz emission.
item.dc:title: Reconstructing into a denser phase
item.dc:creator: Jaewon Wang
Soon-Yong Kwon

item.title: Ideal non-crystals as a distinct form of ordered states without symmetry breaking
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 13 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02496-8

A distinct class of ordered states without symmetry breaking is reported with perfect non-crystalline steric order. These ideal non-crystals exhibit non-trivial long-range orientational correlations and crystal-like properties.
item.dc:title: Ideal non-crystals as a distinct form of ordered states without symmetry breaking
item.dc:creator: Xinyu Fan
Ding Xu
Jianhua Zhang
Hao Hu
Peng Tan
Ning Xu
Hajime Tanaka
Hua Tong

item.title: Helical metasurfaces based on topological surface states in three-dimensional photonic topological insulators
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 12 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02488-8

The open interfaces of a three-dimensional all-dielectric photonic topological insulator operate as effective metasurfaces with helical surface states, enabling spin-dependent steering and directional control of far-field surface state emission.
item.dc:title: Helical metasurfaces based on topological surface states in three-dimensional photonic topological insulators
item.dc:creator: Dmitry V. Zhirihin
Mikhail S. Sidorenko
Alina D. Rozenblit
Georgiy D. Kurganov
Maxim A. Gorlach
Dmitry S. Filonov
Yuri S. Kivshar
Alexey P. Slobozhanyuk

item.title: Cation–polymer interactions drive water expulsion and deswelling in n-type ladder organic mixed conductors
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02478-2

A molecular-level understanding of structural evolution upon electrochemical doping in organic mixed ionic-electronic conductors is required. A pronounced reduction in mass and thickness is observed in an n-type ladder polymer upon doping with protic cations owing to water expulsion.
item.dc:title: Cation–polymer interactions drive water expulsion and deswelling in n-type ladder organic mixed conductors
item.dc:creator: Tom P. A. van der Pol
Dongxun Lyu
Zoé Truyens
Vincent Lemaur
Demetra Tsokkou
Arianna Magni
Chiara Musumeci
Han-Yan Wu
Junpeng Ji
David Cornil
Chi-Yuan Yang
Scott T. Keene
Gabriele D’Avino
Alberto Salleo
Natalie Banerji
Clare Grey
David Beljonne
Simone Fabiano
